name: Jaylen
communication_level: non-verbal, uses PECS (Picture Exchange Communication System) and AAC device
interests: Thomas the Tank Engine (specifically Gordon and Percy)

iep_goals.description: Jaylen will request wants and needs using his AAC device with 80% accuracy across 15 opportunities per day (snack, activity choice, break time, attention, sensory activity) as measured by teacher observation and AAC device log.

trial_history.notes: Relies on pointing and gestures; AAC device less preferred; new speech therapist starting
Using AAC for snack choice consistently; still prefers gesture for most requests
Thomas stickers as reinforcer helping; fire drill disrupted routine but recovered quickly
Consistent AAC use for transitions; staff praising every attempt building confidence
Met target! Using AAC spontaneously for activity choice and sensory break requests

iep_goals.description: Jaylen will follow a visual schedule independently, transitioning between 3-4 activities per day without adult physical prompting, with 90% independence across 5-day weeks, as measured by teacher tally of unprompted transitions.

trial_history.notes: Needs hand-over-hand guidance for most transitions; attached to spinning wheel toy
Visual schedule now includes photos of Jaylen with each activity; slight improvement
Using Thomas sticker chart to track completed transitions; fire drill disrupted but adapted next day
Verbal cue + pointing to schedule sufficient for most transitions; still needs touch prompt occasionally
Met target! Using visual schedule to self-initiate transitions most of the time

iep_goals.description: Jaylen will take turns during preferred activities (turn-taking with spinning top, trains, adult-led games) for 4 out of 5 consecutive opportunities per session, allowing peer/adult turn before requesting another turn, as measured by therapist observation.

trial_history.notes: Resists peer interaction; monopolizes spinning toy; needs adult redirection
Still very resistant; parallel play only; avoids direct turn-taking
1:1 adult-led turn-taking with Thomas trains more successful; peer situation still difficult
Accepting turns with adult present; brief 2-3 second peer exchanges emerging
Met target! Consistently taking turns with adult in preferred activities; beginning peer turn-taking
